sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.73 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: b) Let$G$be a group generated by a finite set$S$, and let$l(g)$denote the word length function of$g G$with respect to$S$. The group$G$is said to be contracting\/ if there exist a faithful action of$G$on the set$X^*$of finite words over a finite alphabet$X$and constants$0< <1$and$C>0$such that for every$g G$and$x X$there exist$h G$and$y X$such that$l(h)< l(g)+C$and$g(xw)=yh(w)$for all$w X^*$.
